chunk:
CHUNK:005/021
\u201cThen we\u2019re babysitting until the Americans arrive.\u201d \u201cWe are,\u201d Gaba said. \u201cUiluiit has to get back to Narsarsuaq, but we can rotate in shifts. Gaba checked the big watch he had strapped to his wrist and said, \u201cSaarullik and Taateraaq have the first shift. I need to check in with the captain. Leaving you free to explore.\u201d \u201cExplore?\u201d I said. \u201cAap.\u201d Gaba nodded in the direction of Qassiarsuk and said, \u201cYou\u2019ve got four hours. You can take the boat into the village, catch up on a bit of Greenland culture, and still get back in time for a nap before relieving Saarullik and Taateraaq. They\u2019re going to allocate us a cabin \u2013 shared, of course.\u201d \u201cOf course,\u201d I said with a shrug. It wouldn\u2019t be the first time. Gaba waited for me to say more, and then followed my gaze as I looked at the door of Rachael\u2019s cabin. \u201cWhat is it, Constable?\u201d \u201cShe was about to say something before you came in,\u201d I said. I tapped my cheek, adding, \u201cAnd she\u2019s been hit. She said it was Sean.\u201d \u201cNot our problem, Petra.\u201d It was the first time he had used my name since leaving Nuuk, and I guessed it was Gaba\u2019s way of softening the blow when he reminded me of the job at hand, and to not let my curiosity twist the operation into something more complicated. \u201cThe Americans are coming,\u201d he said. \u201cLeave it to them.\u201d \u201cWe\u2019re just going to ignore it?\u201d \u201cThey\u2019re criminals,\u201d Gaba said. \u201cDiamond smugglers. They\u2019ve been on the run for six years or more. Granted, tensions are probably high right now \u2013 they\u2019ve been caught, after all. But none of this is our business. We keep them detained, keep them alive, and hand them over as soon as the Americans get here.\u201d Gaba tilted his head to one side to catch my attention as I glanced once more at Rachael\u2019s door. \u201cConstable?\u201d he said. \u201cDo you understand?\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d I said. \u201cGood.\u201d Gaba clicked his fingers and pointed at my gear. \u201cGive me your gun and vest and take the boat into Qassiarsuk. Do the cultural thing. Be back within a couple of hours, then get another couple of hours of rest. Your shift starts at midnight. Got it?\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d I said, as I unsnapped the sling from the MP5. \u201cI got it.\u201d But even if I got it, I knew that my mind would twist in one direction and another as I tried to figure out how to get more. Until, of course, something else came along to distract me. \n 4 I couldn\u2019t decide if it was good news that we had a cabin to rest in, or if the fact that we needed one meant the next however many hours we were babysitting Arnet, and his crew, would be the longest hours of my life. Gaba had obviously been talking with Sergeant Duneq back in Nuuk, as he seemed to have pre-empted any union entanglements by splitting up our shift with break periods. It was a new Gaba, and I wondered if he was bucking for a promotion. But when it came to operational procedure, he had a better grip on the proper way to run an operation, including details like suggesting I leave my ballistic vest and submachine gun in his care before joining throngs of tourists in Qassiarsuk. Okay. Maybe Gaba didn\u2019t use the word throngs, but he was still one step ahead of me. As was Constable Uiluiit when I discovered he was waiting for me on the floating dock tethered to Dreamcaster\u2019s port side. \u201cDo you need a ride?\u201d I asked, as I joined him on the dock. The wind and the waves had died down since we boarded the cruise ship, proving once again that it was Greenland, not Gaba, who had the final say in operational matters. In some areas, at least. \u201cNaamik,\u201d Uiluiit said with a shake of his head. He had thick black hair and a curious scar on his right cheek, just below his eye. He caught me looking and said, \u201cFishhook.\u201d \u201cReally? That must have hurt. Uiluiit laughed and said, \u201cIt did.\u201d The thought of meeting Arnet had distracted me on the upper decks, and I had failed to notice that Uiluiit was only a few years older than me. He was shorter, of course, as many of my colleagues were, but with a wiry build and a badly fitting police jacket that threatened to consume him if he didn\u2019t keep his hands in the pockets, pressing it down. Uiluiit also had a gentle laugh, and I tucked my hands into my pockets and leaned against the railing on the dock for what I hoped was an entertaining story from Uiluiit\u2019s childhood. \u201cIt happened last year,\u201d he said, nodding as I frowned. \u201cAap.\u201d Another nod. \u201cI was chasing a small boy along a beach.\u201d \u201cChasing him?\u201d \u201cSure,\u201d Uiluiit said, dragging the word out as many Greenlanders do. \u201cMakkoq had been a bad boy. He ran away from his anaana, and his uncle, too.\u201d Uiluiit grinned, and I understood. \u201cYou\u2019re the uncle.\u201d \u201cAap,\u201d he said. \u201cAnd Makkoq, like his uncle, and his anaana, is cunning.\u201d \u201cI can imagine,\u201d I said, biting my lip as I leaned into the story. \u201cMakkoq is also very small.\u201d Uiluiit demonstrated, holding his hand flat, just below his waist. \u201cSmall for his age, but fast. He ran onto the beach\u2026\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d I said when Uiluiit paused for effect. \u201cHe ran towards the drying racks.\u201d \u201cFor fish,\u201d I said, nodding as I pictured the spindly wooden structures that could be seen on beaches up and down the Greenland coastline. \u201cFish, shark, seal\u2026\u201d Uiluiit nodded. \u201cThe fishermen dry everything on the racks. Including nets and lines. And Makkoq was just small enough to run under the lines.\u201d \u201cBut not you?\u201d I said.

SCENE:DREAMCASTER-SHIP  
GABA: "WE BABYSIT UNTIL AMERICANS ARRIVE"  
GABA: UILUIIT→RETURNTONARSARSUAQ; SHIFT-ROTATION  
GABA CHECKS WATCH; ASSIGN=SAARULLIK+TAATERAAQ→SHIFT1  
GABA: "NEED CHECK-IN WITH CAPTAIN; YOU FREE TO EXPLORE"  
PETRA: "EXPLORE?"  
GABA: "AAP"  
GABA NODS→QASSIARSUK; "TIME=4H; BOAT→VILLAGE; CULTURE; NAP BEFORE SHIFT2"  
GABA: "CABIN=SHARED"  
PETRA SHRUGS; "OF COURSE"  
PETRA GAZES→RACHAEL-CABIN-DOOR  
GABA: "CONSTABLE?"  
PETRA: "SHE ALMOST SPOKE BEFORE YOU ENTERED; SHE’S BEEN HIT BY SEAN"  
GABA: "NOT OUR PROBLEM PETRA"  
GABA: "AMERICANS COMING; LEAVE IT"  
PETRA: "IGNORE IT?"  
GABA: "THEY=DIAMOND-SMUGGLERS ON-RUN≥6Y; DETENTION→HANDOVER ON US-ARRIVAL"  
GABA TILTS HEAD; "CONSTABLE? UNDERSTAND?"  
PETRA: "YES"  
GABA POINTS→GEAR; "DROP GUN+VEST; BOAT→QASSIARSUK; RETURN=2H; REST=2H; SHIFT2=00:00"  
PETRA: UNSNAPS MP5-SLING; "YES I GOT IT"  
PETRA NOTES: MIND TWISTS→DISTRACTIONS  

SCENE:QASSIARSUK-DOCK  
PETRA DEBATES CABIN-REST VS BABYSITTING-LENGTH  
GABA: COORDINATES WITH SERGEANT-DUNEQ[NUUK]; SHIFT=ROTATED+BREAKS  
UILUIIT WAITS ON DOCK TETHERED→DREAMCASTER-PORT  
PETRA: "NEED A RIDE?"  
WEATHER: WIND+WAVES=CALM  
UILUIIT: "NAAAMIK"  
UILUIIT: HAIR=THICK-BLACK; SCAR=FISHHOOK ON RIGHT-CHEEK  
PETRA: "OUCH?"  
UILUIIT LAUGHS; "IT DID"  
PETRA OBSERVES: AGE≈PETRA+FEW-YR; HEIGHT<AVERAGE; BUILD=WIRY; JACKET=OVERSIZED  
PETRA LEANS→RAILING; HANDS=POCKETED  
UILUIIT: "HAPPENED LAST-YEAR"  
PETRA: "AAP"  
UILUIIT: "I CHASED MAKKOQ ALONG BEACH"  
PETRA: "CHASED?"  
UILUIIT: "SURE; MAKKOQ RAN FROM ANAANA+UNCLE"  
PETRA: "YOU=UNCLE?"  
UILUIIT: "AAP; MAKKOQ+ANAANA+UNCLE=CUNNING"  
PETRA: "MAKKOQ VERY SMALL"  
UILUIIT DEMOS HAND=WAIST-HIGH  
UILUIIT: "SMALL FOR AGE BUT FAST; RAN ONTO BEACH"  
PETRA: "HE RAN TO DRYING-RACKS"  
PETRA: "FOR FISH?"  
UILUIIT NODS; "FISH, SHARK, SEAL..."  
UILUIIT: "FISHERMEN DRY EVERYTHING ON RACKS; NETS+LINES; MAKKOQ RAN UNDER"  
PETRA: "BUT NOT YOU?"
